content.text:
All 3 active tests pass (with 7 todos remaining). The current implementation `return [];` is already the absolute minimal solution that makes this test pass — no changes required.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: No changes needed. The existing `return [];` already satisfies the new test case `nextGeneration([[0,1],[1,1]])` returns `[]`.
- Result: All tests now pass (3 passing, 7 todo).
- Approach: This is the minimal possible implementation — a hardcoded empty array return. Both cells `[0,1]` and `[1,1]` each have exactly one neighbor (the other cell), so by the underpopulation rule they both die, yielding an empty next generation. The hardcoded `[]` covers all three currently-active tests because every active test expects an empty result. Adding any logic now would be over-implementation for tests that don't yet exist.

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-11_01-59-11_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/game-of-life.ts
